name: Devconnect Argentina (The First Ethereum World's Fair)
location: La Rural, Palermo, Buenos Aires, Argentina
attendance: 14,000+ attendees from 130+ countries (45% from Argentina; 53% first-time EF event attendees); 80+ exhibiting teams across 8 districts; 40+ events inside La Rural plus 500+ community-run side events across the city. (Pre-event organizer/press estimates ranged from 10,000-15,000.)
defi_themes: Stablecoins as the breakout / front-end story of crypto (on-the-ground payments in BA, inflation hedge in Argentina ~19.8% crypto ownership)
Argentine peso-pegged stablecoin wARS launched on Ethereum, Base and World Chain
Consumer-facing DeFi / bringing direct DeFi access to retail (Aave App launch)
On-chain credit and lending markets evolution (Aave V4, Morpho, Gearbox, Spark, Cap)
Institutional DeFi and institutional adoption (DeFi-ing Institutions; institutional privacy on Ethereum)
Unified / defragmented liquidity and capital efficiency (1inch Aqua, Fluid DEX v2, CoWSwap cross-chain auctions, RFQs vs AMMs)
Restaking / liquid restaking and verifiable yield (EtherFi, bond.credit)
DeFAI - AI agents in DeFi (autonomous trading, agent-managed stablecoin strategies, verifiable yield, prediction agents)
DeFi security: oracle manipulation, governance attack vectors, war rooms / incident response, ERC-4626 / ERC-4337 / EIP-7702 risks, MEV (multisig MEV via Safe Harbour)
DeFi regulation and policy (EU MiCA-era policy discussion - 'What we talk about when we talk about DeFi in Europe')
Stablecoin design and stablecoin banking (M0, resilient stablecoin design)

notable_speakers.name: Oskar Thoren
notable_speakers.affiliation: Ethereum Foundation (Lead, Institutional Privacy Task Force)

key_takeaways: Stablecoins were the breakout story of Devconnect ARG: they were used on the ground in Buenos Aires (paying for food/services in crypto), framed as the 'front end of crypto' and an inflation 'escape hatch' for Argentina (~19.8% crypto ownership; ARS down ~99% vs USD over a decade). A new peso-pegged stablecoin, wARS, launched on Ethereum, Base and World Chain.

Consumer DeFi push: Aave launched the new unified Aave App (iOS), with Stani Kulechov arguing DeFi must move beyond savvy/professional users to bring direct access to consumers (high-yield savings framing).
On-chain credit/lending was a central theme - Aave V4, Morpho, Gearbox, Spark, Cap and others featured across both 'DeFi Today' (Evolution of On-Chain Credit Markets) and the DeFi Security Summit.

RWA, fund tokenization and institutional DeFi were headline tracks at the dedicated 'DeFi Today' event (Danny Ryan's 'DeFi-ing Institutions', fund tokenization, institutional privacy on Ethereum led by the EF's Institutional Privacy Task Force).

Liquidity defragmentation / capital efficiency emerged as a 2026 narrative: 1inch Aqua (defragmenting liquidity for market makers), Fluid DEX v2, CoW Swap's cross-chain single-auction vision, and RFQ-vs-AMM debates.
DeFAI (AI agents in DeFi) was a strong sub-theme: autonomous trading, agent-managed stablecoin strategies, prediction agents and 'verifiable yield', alongside heavy attention to its security risks.

DeFi security maturity: the 4th DeFi Security Summit (Nov 20-21, ~1,000+ attendees) emphasized oracle security, governance-as-attack-vector, war rooms/incident response, ERC-4626/ERC-4337/EIP-7702 risks, and the annual 'State of DeFi Security' review.

Overall framing for 2026: 'infrastructure story instead of casino story,' stablecoins as the consumer front-end, privacy as table stakes, and DeFi positioning toward real-world utility and institutions. The World's Fair had a dedicated DeFi district among its eight zones.
